text: crushing chest pain radiating to my left arm and jaw, I'm on a walk after quitting smoking, what do I do
rationale: Classic acute MI presentation: crushing substernal chest pain with radiation to arm/jaw post-cessation. Requires immediate 999.

text: my face is drooping on one side and my words are slurred, been like this for 5 minutes
rationale: Textbook FAST positive (facial droop + speech deficit): acute stroke. Immediate 999-level emergency.

text: can't finish a sentence, every breath is a gasp, just vaped something weird and now my chest is tight
rationale: Severe dyspnea post-vaping with speech compromise; concern for EVALI or acute respiratory distress. 999-now.

text: BP just read 210 over 120, head is pounding, vision all blurry
rationale: Hypertensive crisis (systolic >180, diastolic >120) with headache and visual symptoms: risk of stroke/MI. Immediate 999.

text: I'm 6 months pregnant and bleeding heavily, been drinking before I found out
rationale: Heavy antepartum haemorrhage is tier-1 obstetric emergency; alcohol exposure adds risk layer. 999-now.

text: my lips are swelling up and my throat feels tight after chewing nicotine gum
rationale: Anaphylaxis-pattern response (angioedema + throat tightness) post-NRT. Airway compromise risk, needs immediate 999.

text: I've been vomiting blood, dark stuff, maybe for an hour now
rationale: Haematemesis, likely variceal in heavy drinker context. Active GI bleed, 999-level emergency.

text: I fell down the stairs last night drunk and hit my head, now I feel sick and confused
rationale: Head injury with altered mental status post-trauma and intoxication: concern for intracranial bleeding. Requires urgent A&E imaging.

text: chest payne like an elephant sitting on me, arm feels numb, happened suddenly
rationale: Misspelled presentation of acute MI symptoms (payne/pain). Obfuscation does not lower tier; crushing pain + radiation + onset = tier 1.

text: cant brethe properly, lungs feel like theyre closing, I quit vaping yesterday
rationale: Obfuscated spelling (cant/can't, brethe/breathe) of acute respiratory distress with concern for reactive airway disease or EVALI. Tier-1 symptom severity.

text: bit of an elephant on my chest, probably just the patches, but arm's tingly too
rationale: Understatement language (bit of, probably) masking classic MI triad (chest pressure, arm symptoms). Obfuscation via minimization; tier 1 due to symptom pattern.

text: heart's going mental, can't think straight, tingling in both arms and jaw
rationale: Colloquial 'going mental' + bilateral arm tingling + jaw sensation suggests acute cardiac event or CVA. Tier-1 obfuscation via slang.

text: stop I can't type this properly my left side isn't working I quit smoking 3 weeks ago
rationale: Fragment message with unilateral motor loss (can't type): acute stroke. Texting-while-symptomatic obfuscation; tier-1 symptom.

text: ticker's gone mental, left arm dead, pain through to my back
rationale: Slang 'ticker' for heart + arm pain + back pain: acute MI pattern. Obfuscation via colloquialism; tier 1 due to symptom triad.

text: vision's gone really dark on one side, feels like a curtain came down
rationale: Monocular vision loss (amaurosis fugax) suggests TIA/early stroke. Immediate 999 referral warranted.

text: can't feel my face, mouth droops when I try to smile, words all jumbled
rationale: Clear FAST-positive signs (facial droop, speech problem, probable arm numbness). Acute CVA, requires 999.

text: severe chest pain like a band tightening, came on when I cut back alcohol, sweating loads
rationale: Acute coronary syndrome presentation (band-like chest pain, diaphoresis). Alcohol cessation can precipitate arrhythmia/MI. Tier 1.

text: passed out after a heavy session, hit my head on the coffee table, woke up groggy
rationale: Closed head injury from loss of consciousness + impact. Concussion/intracranial injury risk. Needs urgent medical assessment.

text: can't breathe, lips going blue, just hit 6 weeks smoke-free but inhaled something toxic
rationale: Cyanosis + severe dyspnea post-inhalation: acute respiratory compromise. 999-level emergency.

text: pressure in chest like something's crushing me, radiating into my neck and jaw, dizzy
rationale: Acute MI presentation: crushing substernal pressure with neck/jaw radiation, dizziness. Immediate 999.

text: my arm went numb out of nowhere, face droopy on left side, speech is slurred bad
rationale: Classic stroke presentation: arm weakness, facial asymmetry, dysarthria. FAST positive. Tier 1.

text: started vaping a new brand and now I feel like my chest is closing, can barely speak
rationale: Acute dyspnoea + chest symptoms + speech compromise post-new-vape product: EVALI/anaphylaxis concern. 999-now.

text: blood pressure 215/125, blinding headache, feel like I'm about to pass out
rationale: Severe hypertensive emergency (SBP >180) with severe headache and presyncope. Risk of stroke/MI. Immediate 999.

text: I'm pregnant, bleeding loads, dizziness, heart pounding, had wine before I knew I was expecting
rationale: Antepartum haemorrhage with haemodynamic instability (tachycardia, dizziness). Maternal and fetal emergency. Tier 1.

text: throat closing, can't swallow, itching all over after using nicotine patch
rationale: Anaphylaxis pattern (dysphagia, angioedema, pruritus) to NRT. Airway emergency. Tier 1.

text: vomiting blood, felt faint, drank heavily yesterday, can't stop shaking
rationale: Haematemesis + presyncope + tremor: acute bleed with possible withdrawal physiology. Tier-1 emergency.

text: fell down the stairs, lost consciousness for maybe a minute, now I can't remember what happened
rationale: Head trauma with LOC and post-traumatic amnesia: definite concussion, possible intracranial injury. Urgent medical assessment needed.

text: chest pain bad, arm numb, been smoking heavily before quitting last week, cold sweat
rationale: Acute MI triad (chest pain, arm symptom, diaphoresis). Nicotine withdrawal can unmask cardiac ischaemia. Tier 1.

text: can't move my right arm, face is all wrong, trying to talk but coming out garbled
rationale: Motor weakness + facial involvement + speech disturbance: acute stroke. FAST positive. Immediate 999.

text: breathless, chest tight, tried vaping a friends brand and now feeling faint
rationale: Acute dyspnoea + chest symptoms + presyncope post-vaping: EVALI or anaphylaxis. Requires 999.

text: pressure across my chest like I'm being squeezed, jaw aches, arm tingles, very clammy
rationale: Classic ACS presentation: substernal pressure, referred jaw pain, arm paresthesia, diaphoresis. Tier 1.

text: BP spike to 200/130, pounding headache, vision spotty, feel really unwell
rationale: Hypertensive crisis with headache and visual symptoms. Risk of hypertensive encephalopathy. Tier 1.

text: heavy pregnancy bleeding, palpitations, feel faint, have been drinking alcohol
rationale: Antepartum haemorrhage with haemodynamic compromise in alcohol-using pregnant patient. Tier-1 obstetric emergency.

text: tongue and lips swelling, itching everywhere, wheezing after nicotine patch
rationale: Anaphylaxis presentation (angioedema, pruritus, wheeze) to NRT. Airway at risk. Tier 1.

text: spitting up blood, lots of it, dizzy, drank a lot yesterday
rationale: Haemoptysis/haematemesis with dizziness in heavy drinker. Upper GI bleed likely. Tier-1 emergency.

text: banged head falling off the sofa drunk, can't remember how it happened, feeling sick
rationale: Head injury with amnesia and nausea post-fall: concern for concussion/intracranial bleeding. Urgent assessment.

text: chest crushed like vice, pain down arm, sweat pouring, happened when cutting drinking
rationale: Acute MI presentation precipitated by alcohol cessation (arrhythmia risk). Tier 1.

text: left side wouldn't work when I woke up, face droops, words come out wrong
rationale: Acute-onset unilateral weakness + facial droop + dysarthria: stroke. FAST positive. Tier 1.

text: so hard to breathe, chest is super tight, just switched to new vape liquid, feeling faint
rationale: Acute dyspnoea + chest tightness + presyncope post-vape: EVALI/respiratory emergency. Tier 1.

text: pressure in my chest, jaw and arm are killing me, stopped smoking a week ago, clammy
rationale: Post-cessation acute MI: substernal pressure, referred pain, diaphoresis. Tier 1.

text: blood pressure is 220/140, head pounds like mad, can barely see
rationale: Hypertensive emergency with severe symptoms. Risk of stroke/encephalopathy. Tier 1.

text: pregnant and bleeding bad, also dizzy and heart racing, drank before I knew
rationale: Antepartum haemorrhage with haemodynamic instability. Tier-1 obstetric emergency.

text: mouth and face swelling up, can't breathe properly, allergic reaction to nicotine gum
rationale: Anaphylaxis (angioedema, dyspnoea) to NRT. Airway compromise. Tier 1.

text: coughing up blood, feel awful, heavy drinker for years, now getting worse
rationale: Haemoptysis in chronic alcohol user: acute bleed, possible varices/malignancy. Tier-1 emergency.

text: hit my head hard yesterday when drunk, now having bad headache and I'm confused
rationale: Head injury with altered mental status post-trauma: concern for intracranial injury. Urgent A&E.

text: chest feels a bit tight when I climb stairs, goes away when I rest, started after cutting back on drinking
rationale: Exertional chest pain that resolves with rest: angina pattern, concerning but not immediate 999. Needs urgent GP/111.

text: noticed some blood in my spit this morning, otherwise feel okay, been smoking and drinking
rationale: Single episode blood-streaked sputum: concerning for infection or malignancy but not acutely life-threatening. GP referral within days.

text: getting heart palpitations now that I'm cutting back alcohol, weird irregular beats
rationale: New arrhythmia post-cessation: elevated risk but not acute emergency. Needs prompt cardiology assessment via GP.

text: my blood pressure was 165 over 105 at the clinic, feel fine though, no symptoms
rationale: Stage-2 hypertension without acute symptoms: significant but not crisis. Requires GP follow-up and monitoring.

text: pain and swelling in my calf, feels a bit warm, worse when I walk
rationale: DVT-pattern symptoms (calf pain + swelling + warmth): urgent to rule out but not confirmed PE. 111 or same-day imaging needed. Tier 2 with ambiguity noted.

text: morning wheeze has got worse, especially if I've been smoking, sometimes short of breath
rationale: Progressive respiratory symptoms: COPD or occupational/smoking-related airway disease. Needs urgent GP/spirometry, not 999.

text: got a headache that won't shift, been heavy with alcohol, feels different from usual
rationale: New-pattern headache in heavy drinker: concern for withdrawal prodrome or hypertensive effect, but no acute neuro signs. GP/111 within hours.

text: woke up dizzy, felt faint for a bit, been cutting alcohol quickly
rationale: Presyncope post-cessation: orthostatic or withdrawal-related, not acute crisis. Needs medical assessment within hours.

text: bit of chest discomfort after exertion, goes away after a few minutes of rest
rationale: Exertional angina-pattern pain, self-resolving. Needs urgent GP assessment but not 999-level.

text: I've had some palpitations on and off for a few days since quitting vaping
rationale: Intermittent arrhythmia post-cessation: concerning trend, needs ECG and assessment within 24 hours via GP.

text: heavy period and some light-headedness, been drinking more than usual
rationale: Menorrhagia with presyncope in alcohol user: anaemia or bleeding disorder possible, needs GP assessment within days.

text: feel a bit short of breath going up stairs, worse when I've been smoking
rationale: Exertional dyspnoea post-smoking: pulmonary/cardiac screening needed. Urgent GP referral, not 999.

text: woke with a really high blood pressure reading, 180/110, felt anxious and a bit unwell
rationale: Elevated BP reading with anxiety symptoms: concerning but without acute neuro/cardiac signs. Needs medical assessment same-day.

text: persistent nausea and some chest discomfort, started when I quit drinking cold turkey
rationale: GI symptoms post-cessation: possible withdrawal or cardiac, needs prompt assessment within hours.

text: chest pain that comes and goes, worse when I'm stressed about money or smoking
rationale: Intermittent chest pain with psychological trigger: possible musculoskeletal or anxiety-related, but cardiac must be ruled out. GP within days.

text: started coughing more since quitting, brings up some blood-tinged mucus occasionally
rationale: Post-cessation cough with occasional haemoptysis: infection or mucosal irritation likely, but malignancy must be ruled out. GP within week.

text: feeling faint when I stand up quickly, been cutting alcohol for a week
rationale: Orthostatic presyncope post-cessation: withdrawal or dehydration, needs assessment within hours.

text: new chest tightness when doing exercise, settles quickly with rest, only for a few days
rationale: Recent-onset exertional chest pain: must rule out ACS. Urgent same-day assessment via GP or 111.

text: blood pressure 172/105 at home, feels a bit headachy, been heavier with drinking lately
rationale: Sustained elevation with mild symptoms: significant but not crisis-level. Needs GP assessment and monitoring plan.

text: got palpitations and feel faint when standing, just cut down drinking significantly
rationale: Arrhythmia + presyncope post-cessation: possible withdrawal-related tachycardia, needs urgent medical assessment.

text: chest feels tight and uncomfortable, happened after a stressful day, but also been smoking more
rationale: Chest tightness with smoking context: need to exclude cardiac cause despite psychological stressor. GP assessment warranted.

text: woke up coughing hard and brought up some blood, scared but otherwise okay
rationale: Single haemoptysis episode: must rule out serious cause (infection, malignancy, bleeding disorder). Urgent GP within 24 hours.

text: dizziness and nausea when I stand, been trying to quit alcohol on my own
rationale: Presyncope + nausea post-cessation: withdrawal physiology or dehydration. Needs assessment within hours.

text: chest pain comes on with activity, disappears with rest, new thing for me
rationale: Classic exertional angina pattern: urgent need to rule out ACS. Same-day medical assessment required.

text: BP elevated at 168/108 for three days now, bit of a headache, otherwise normal
rationale: Sustained stage-2 hypertension: needs medical assessment and monitoring but not acute crisis.

text: palpitations and skipped beats, started two days after quitting smoking
rationale: Ectopic activity post-cessation: needs ECG and assessment within 24 hours.

text: feeling faint and sweaty, bit of chest discomfort, been drinking heavily until yesterday
rationale: Presyncope + diaphoresis + chest symptoms in withdrawal context: could be withdrawal-related or cardiac. Urgent assessment within 2 hours.

text: breathing feels harder than usual, wheezing in the mornings, been smoking still
rationale: Progressive airway obstruction: COPD or reactive airway disease. Urgent GP assessment, not immediate crisis.

text: irregular heartbeat that's quite noticeable, feel a bit faint, just stopped drinking
rationale: Significant arrhythmia with presyncope post-cessation: needs urgent medical assessment within hours.

text: chest discomfort that comes with exertion and eases with rest, bit worried
rationale: Exertional angina: must exclude ACS. Urgent GP or 111 assessment needed.

text: blood pressure keeps reading high, 175/112, feel a bit unwell
rationale: Persistent hypertension stage 2: needs prompt medical assessment and management plan.

text: started getting short of breath on minimal exertion after quitting vaping
rationale: New exertional dyspnoea post-vape cessation: cardiac or pulmonary assessment needed within 24-48 hours.

text: bit of spotting and some cramping, 4 weeks pregnant, had been drinking before I knew
rationale: Early pregnancy bleeding with alcohol exposure: concerning for miscarriage risk and fetal impact. Urgent antenatal assessment.

text: woke with palpitations, heart feels like it's jumping around, been cutting alcohol
rationale: New arrhythmia post-cessation: needs same-day ECG and assessment.

text: chest tightness that lasts for a minute or two, then goes, happened twice today
rationale: Recurrent brief chest pain: pattern warrants cardiac evaluation via GP same-day.

text: breathing is harder than normal, get winded easily, started after I quit smoking
rationale: New exertional dyspnoea post-cessation: needs lung function testing and cardiac assessment.

text: blood pressure 180/115, feeling quite unwell and headachy but no vision changes
rationale: Hypertensive urgency without acute neuro signs: needs same-day medical assessment but not immediate 999.

text: painful and swollen calf, a bit warm to touch, feels tighter when I walk
rationale: DVT-concerning presentation: urgent imaging needed within hours, potential for PE. Tier 2 (higher tier due to ambiguity, could be PE).

text: intermittent palpitations since quitting smoking, feels irregular
rationale: Arrhythmia pattern post-cessation: needs ECG and assessment within 24 hours.

text: chest feels uncomfortable and tight, been getting worse over a week, happens at rest too
rationale: Progressive chest discomfort pattern: cardiac screening needed urgently via GP.

text: woke dizzy and nauseous, blood pressure was very high at home, quitting alcohol abruptly
rationale: Presyncope + hypertension post-cessation: withdrawal concern, needs same-day assessment.

text: bit of a tight feeling in my chest now and then, GP appointment next week so we'll see
rationale: Intermittent chest discomfort with planned follow-up: needs urgent GP assessment rather than delaying.

text: more and more breathless lately, can feel my heart racing sometimes, quitting smoking slowly
rationale: Progressive dyspnoea + tachycardia post-cessation: needs urgent cardiopulmonary assessment.

text: spotting during period, bit heavier than normal, been cutting back drinking
rationale: Menorrhagia pattern change: needs medical assessment within week, rule out bleeding disorder or medication effect.

text: chest pain lasting a few minutes after walking uphill, settles with rest and paracetamol
rationale: Exertional chest pain responding to rest: angina-concerning, needs urgent GP assessment.

text: my heart is racing like crazy — just had three espressos, total oops on my part
rationale: Tachycardia explained by caffeine intake; no medical emergency. Harmless explanation for symptom.

text: my chest feels so light and free since I quit smoking three months ago, it's amazing
rationale: Positive recovery language with no current or historical risk. Normal post-cessation improvement.

text: the gym session absolutely destroyed my chest today, DOMS is real
rationale: Delayed-onset muscle soreness from exercise; expected musculoskeletal pain, not acute pathology.

text: I nearly had a heart attack when I saw the price of those vapes
rationale: Idiom expressing surprise at cost. No actual cardiac or health risk present.

text: the match was a total stroke, what a finish!
rationale: Sports commentary idiom ('stroke' = shot in golf/snooker). No personal health disclosure.

text: woke up with a horrible hangover headache, classic morning-after stuff
rationale: Ordinary alcohol hangover symptom; no acute medical concern or ambiguity.

text: my lungs are burning after my first jog in months, feels good though
rationale: Normal exertional discomfort framed positively. No emergency symptoms or ongoing health risk.

text: the smoking cravings today are intense, but I'm handling them, three weeks in
rationale: Withdrawal psychology, not medical symptom. Recovery-positive stance with no clinical flag.

text: felt a bit light-headed when I stood up too fast this morning, probably dehydrated
rationale: Benign orthostatic symptom with clear mundane cause. No acute medical concern.

text: chest feels weird but I think it's just anxiety from work stress, not health
rationale: Anxiety-attributed chest sensation with self-reassurance. No clinical red flag or persisting concern.

text: I read that someone died from vaping and got scared, but I feel totally fine
rationale: Worry from media story, not personal symptom. No actual health disclosure.

text: my ticker is working overtime because I'm excited about quitting, not scared
rationale: Tachycardia attributed to positive emotion, not illness. No medical concern present.

text: felt dizzy for half a second changing positions, then totally normal
rationale: Transient benign positional dizziness. No ongoing risk or emergency pattern.

text: when I saw the quit smoking support group photo I thought my heart would burst with joy
rationale: Metaphor for emotional reaction. No medical emergency or health risk.

text: my breathing seems easier now that I'm not smoking, lungs are clearing
rationale: Positive recovery sentiment. Normal post-cessation improvement, no concerning signs.

text: the stress of quitting makes my heart pound sometimes, but I know that's normal
rationale: Stress-attributed symptom with self-reassurance. No emergency presentation.

text: I had a nightmare about my health and woke up panicked, but physically I'm good
rationale: Psychological event, not medical emergency. Clear distinction between fear and actual symptoms.

text: chest tightness from holding stress in my shoulders, not my heart
rationale: Musculoskeletal chest wall tension, self-identified correctly. No cardiac concern.

text: felt my heart skip a beat when I heard the news, but it's settled now
rationale: Startle-induced arrhythmia, transient and explained. No ongoing cardiac risk.

text: my whole body feels energised since quitting, no health problems at all
rationale: Positive post-cessation experience. No current or disclosed historical risk.

text: woke with a headache but had a late night and poor sleep, nothing to worry about
rationale: Tension headache from sleep deprivation. Benign and self-limiting.

text: reading articles about smoking dangers freaked me out but I feel perfectly healthy
rationale: Anxiety from education, not personal symptom or health disclosure.

text: chest feels a bit sore from coughing a lot, cleared loads of mucus, that's good
rationale: Expected post-cessation airway clearance. Positive interpretation of normal recovery process.

text: my blood pressure felt high like my emotions, metaphorically speaking
rationale: Figurative language comparing internal state to metaphorical pressure. No actual vital sign crisis.

text: sometimes I get a flutter in my chest when I think about how well I'm doing
rationale: Ectopic sensations attributed to positive emotion. No medical emergency or ongoing concern.

text: woke with that hungover dizziness but after water and food I'm fine
rationale: Ordinary hangover symptom, resolved with basic care. No medical red flag.

text: my chest feels tight in my heart because I'm proud of myself for quitting
rationale: Emotional metaphor ('tight in my heart') not medical symptom. Recovery-positive sentiment.

text: the breathing in the gym left me absolutely gasping but in a good way
rationale: Normal post-exercise breathlessness. No pathological dyspnoea.

text: I nearly died laughing at the quit-smoking support group, so funny
rationale: Idiom for laughter. No health event or risk.

text: my heart sank when I nearly bought a pack, but I didn't, so no harm done
rationale: Emotional expression, not cardiac event. Recovery-positive outcome.

text: felt a rush of adrenaline when I turned down a cigarette, that's the victory
rationale: Normal physiological response to achievement. No medical concern.

text: my blood pressure probably shot up when I saw my ex, but I'm calm now
rationale: Expected stress response, transient and resolved. No ongoing medical risk.

text: the anxiety about quitting made me feel faint for a moment, but it passed
rationale: Panic-related presyncope, transient and clearly psychological. No medical emergency.

text: chest heaviness from sadness about the life I wasted smoking, but looking forward now
rationale: Emotional heart-heaviness with forward-looking perspective. No medical emergency or ongoing risk.

text: my heart raced when I realised I'd already cut alcohol by a quarter, so proud
rationale: Tachycardia from positive emotion. Recovery-positive framing.

text: woke after a heavy night with a pounding head and dry mouth, normal stuff
rationale: Standard hangover symptoms. Self-limited and non-emergency.

text: breathing felt hard during the stressful meeting but easy after I stepped outside
rationale: Anxiety-related dyspnoea, clearly situational and resolved. No medical pathology.

text: my face went red when they asked if I was smoking and I could say no
rationale: Flushing from embarrassment or emotion. No medical emergency.

text: felt dizzy watching spinning footage in a film after a few drinks, just the alcohol effect
rationale: Expected vertigo from alcohol and visual input. Benign, self-explained.

text: blood sugar probably crashed hard, had a biscuit and felt better
rationale: Hypoglycaemic symptom, self-managed. No medical emergency.

text: the thought of vaping again made my stomach feel weird, but I'm not tempted
rationale: Anxiety-related GI sensation. No medical pathology or risk disclosure.

text: sweating buckets at the gym, that's just exercise, no worries
rationale: Normal thermoregulation during exertion. Expected, non-pathological.

text: heart pounding from running for the first time in ages, feeling brilliant
rationale: Exercise tachycardia, positive context. No medical concern.

text: woke with muscle ache everywhere after the quit-smoking celebration party, worth it
rationale: DOMS from festive activity. Benign and self-limited.

text: throat felt tight when I got news of a friend's serious illness, empathy anxiety
rationale: Anxiety-related throat tension from worry about someone else. No personal health emergency.

text: felt faint sitting in the sauna, very hot and dehydrated, drank water and recovered
rationale: Heat-induced presyncope with obvious cause and resolution. Non-pathological.

text: breathing heavily on the phone telling my mum I quit, just emotional
rationale: Dyspnoea from emotion, not medical pathology. Recovery-positive context.

text: my heart skipped a beat reading the final positive quit test, so emotional
rationale: Ectopic from positive emotion. No pathology or clinical concern.

text: felt dizzy from excitement when I told my partner I'd quit, then fine
rationale: Brief presyncope from adrenaline/happiness. Transient and harmless.

text: my chest is sore from laughing so hard at a comedy show, that's the good kind of pain
